content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
June 21,
1996
Thomas F.
Seymour
P.O.
Box 7007
15625 Stafford Street, Suite 203
City of Industry,
CA 91744
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-96—195
Dear Mr.
Seymour:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding your filing obligations under the conflict of interest
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
QUESTION
Are you obligated to a file a Statement of Economic Interests
(Form 730),
as a member of the Reporting Quality Monitoring
Committee of the State Board of Accountancy?
CONCLUSION
No.
You are under no obligation to file a Form 730.
FACTS
You are an appointed member of the Reporting Quality
Monitoring Committee of the State Board of Accountancy.
You
received a letter dated May 6,
1996,
from the Department of
Consumer Affairs stating that the Department had amended its
conflict of interest code on May 1,
1996,
and that you are
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
A2
T
,,,,, Qflfl
V
I’
- ncOl A
tfll


Seymour,
Thomas F.
Our File No. A-96-195
Page2
obligated to file a Form 730 pursuant to your service on that
committee.
A review of the conflict of interest code for the Department
of Consumer Affairs shows that the current code does not require
members of the Reporting Quality Monitoring Committee of the State
Board of Accountancy to file Form 730.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires individuals who make or participate in the
making of governmental decisions to file statements of economic
interests.
Statements of economic interests require public
officials and employees of public agencies to disclose sources of
income, business positions,
investments, -interests in real
property and gifts which may give rise to a conflict of interest
in the performance of their official duties.
Section 87300 requires every public agency,
including state
agencies, to adopt a conflict of interest code.
The conflict of
interest code must specify which employees and individuals who
perform work for, or who provide advice or recommendations to the
agency are deemed to make or participate in the making of
governmental decisions and must require those individuals to file•
periodic statements of economic interests.
Since the Reporting Quality Monitoring Committee of the State
Board of Accountancy is not mentioned in the list of designated
positions in the conflict of interest code of the Department of
Consumer Affairs, you have no obligation to file a statement of
economic interests.
If you have additional questions, please contact me at
(916)
322—5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Coun
By:
Wayne P.
Imberi
Plitical Reform Consultant


Robinson, Barbara
Department of Consumer Affairs
Page
2
If I can be of assistance, please contact me at
(916)
322—
5660.
Sincerely,
Jt
Wayne
linberi
Political Reform Consultant
cc:
Marjorie M.
Berte
Director,
Department of Consumer Affairs
Carol Sigmann
Executive Officer,
Board of Accountancy
Daniel Buntjer
Supervising Counsel,
Department of Consumer Affairs

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are you obligated to a file a Statement of Economic Interests
(Form 730),
as a member of the Reporting Quality Monitoring
Committee of the State Board of Accountancy?

CONCLUSION: No.
You are under no obligation to file a Form 730.
